summary: Typosquat of codebuff CLI fetching binaries from an unrelated personal GitHub account
details: The package name `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate `codebuff` CLI. README.md is copied verbatim from CodebuffAI/codebuff (including `codebuff.com` links, `support@codebuff.com`, and `npm install -g codebuff` instructions), but the package is published under an unaffiliated name and its bin entry downloads native binaries from an unrelated personal GitHub account, `Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli`. In `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs`, lines 10-11 set `REPO = 'Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'` and resolve `releases/latest` (a mutable tag); lines 167-179 download `${baseUrl}/${platformName}` with no hash or signature check; line 187 runs `fs.chmodSync(destPath, 0o755)`; line 193 spawns the binary with the user's argv via `spawn(binaryPath, process.argv.slice(2), {stdio:'inherit'})`. The combination of name impersonation, plagiarized README directing users to run the CLI under a trusted brand, mutable `latest` release tag, mismatched publisher, and absence of integrity verification means the author can ship arbitrary native code to anyone who installs and runs this CLI. Trigger is at CLI invocation rather than install time, but the README is engineered to lead developers to run it under the belief that they are using the legitimate codebuff tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
